title: eriogonum × nebraskense
intro: Eriogonum × nebraskense, also known as Nebraska sulphur flower, is a hybrid wildflower species that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically its roots and leaves, which contain alkaloids and other compounds with potential health benefits. The roots and leaves of Eriogonum × nebraskense have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Eriogonum × nebraskense can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its effectiveness and safety.
